第59回（1953rd year ）内宮式年遷宮]] | caption = The transfer ceremony at Ise Grand Shrine during the 59th Shikinen Sengu (1953) | description = A sacred Shinto ritual transferring the deity from an old shrine building to a new one. | associated_shrine = [[Ise Grand Shrine]] | related_event = [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] }} '''Sengyo''' (遷御), or '''Sengyo no Gi''' (遷御の儀), is a crucial [[Shinto]] ritual in the context of the [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] ceremonies at the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It involves the solemn transfer of the deity from the old shrine building to the newly reconstructed one, marking the climax of the Sengu ceremony.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 21-22[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]], 1986, p. 58 == Overview == While general Shinto rites at Ise follow the "Geku-sen-sai" (Outer Shrine first) principle, the Sengyo of the Shikinen Sengu is conducted first at the [[Kōtai Jingū|Naiku]] (Inner Shrine) and then at the [[:en:Geku|Geku]] (Outer Shrine).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai, 1990, p. 150 == Ritual Schedule == Since the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Sengyo was traditionally held on the 17th day of the 9th lunar month. However, in modern times, the date is determined by the Emperor of Japan.Sakurai, 1970, p. 193 Typically, the Naiku ceremony occurs on [[:en:October 2|October 2]], and the Geku ceremony on [[:en:October 5|October 5]], starting at 8 PM.Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 16 == The Ceremony == During Sengyo, the sacred object representing the deity is moved in complete darkness, symbolizing a return to the primordial state before creation. This process involves: * '''Chanting of sacred words''' to mark the deity’s departure from the old shrine. * A '''procession under special canopies''' with accompanying rituals and music. * '''Re-enshrinement''' in the new shrine, with the offering of sacred items and prayers. == Media Coverage == The Sengyo ceremonies are significant cultural events in Japan, often covered extensively by the media. Notably, the 62nd Shikinen Sengu (2013) was broadcast live, reflecting its importance to Japanese cultural heritage.Asahi Shimbun, 2013 == See Also == * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.sengu.info/index.html Ise Shikinen Sengu Official Site] * [http://www.sengu.info/video_sengyo/ Video Footage of Sengyo] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|遷御|version=99285933|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:遷御]] [[Category:October]] [[Category:Festivals in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto festivals]] {{wikidata link|Q24892914}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

Notably, the 62nd Shikinen Sengu (2013) was broadcast live, reflecting its importance to Japanese cultural heritage.Asahi Shimbun, 2013 == See Also == * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.sengu.info/index.html Ise Shikinen Sengu Official Site] * [http://www.sengu.info/video_sengyo/ Video Footage of Sengyo] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|遷御|version=99285933|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:遷御]] {{wikidata link|Q24892914}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Festivals in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:October]] [[Category:Shinto festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

第59回（1953rd year ）内宮式年遷宮]] | caption = The transfer ceremony at Ise Grand Shrine during the 59th Shikinen Sengu (1953) | description = A sacred Shinto ritual transferring the deity from an old shrine building to a new one. | associated_shrine = [[Ise Grand Shrine]] | related_event = [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] }} '''Sengyo''' (遷御), or '''Sengyo no Gi''' (遷御の儀), is a crucial [[Shinto]] ritual in the context of the [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] ceremonies at the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It involves the solemn transfer of the deity from the old shrine building to the newly reconstructed one, marking the climax of the Sengu ceremony.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 21-22[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]], 1986, p. 58 == Overview == While general Shinto rites at Ise follow the "Geku-sen-sai" (Outer Shrine first) principle, the Sengyo of the Shikinen Sengu is conducted first at the [[Kōtai Jingū|Naiku]] (Inner Shrine) and then at the [[:en:Geku|Geku]] (Outer Shrine).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai, 1990, p. 150 == Ritual Schedule == Since the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Sengyo was traditionally held on the 17th day of the 9th lunar month. However, in modern times, the date is determined by the Emperor of Japan.Sakurai, 1970, p. 193 Typically, the Naiku ceremony occurs on [[:en:October 2|October 2]], and the Geku ceremony on [[:en:October 5|October 5]], starting at 8 PM.Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 16 == The Ceremony == During Sengyo, the sacred object representing the deity is moved in complete darkness, symbolizing a return to the primordial state before creation. This process involves: * '''Chanting of sacred words''' to mark the deity’s departure from the old shrine. * A '''procession under special canopies''' with accompanying rituals and music. * '''Re-enshrinement''' in the new shrine, with the offering of sacred items and prayers. == Media Coverage == The Sengyo ceremonies are significant cultural events in Japan, often covered extensively by the media. Notably, the 62nd Shikinen Sengu (2013) was broadcast live, reflecting its importance to Japanese cultural heritage.Asahi Shimbun, 2013 == See Also == * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.sengu.info/index.html Ise Shikinen Sengu Official Site] * [http://www.sengu.info/video_sengyo/ Video Footage of Sengyo] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|遷御|version=99285933|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:遷御]] {{wikidata link|Q24892914}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Festivals in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:October]] [[Category:Shinto festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It involves the solemn transfer of the deity from the old shrine building to the newly reconstructed one, marking the climax of the Sengu ceremony.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 21-22{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University}}, 1986, p. 58 == Overview == While general Shinto rites at Ise follow the "Geku-sen-sai" (Outer Shrine first) principle, the Sengyo of the Shikinen Sengu is conducted first at the [[Kōtai Jingū|Naiku]] (Inner Shrine) and then at the [[:en:Geku|Geku]] (Outer Shrine).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai, 1990, p. 150 == Ritual Schedule == Since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the Sengyo was traditionally held on the 17th day of the 9th lunar month. However, in modern times, the date is determined by the Emperor of Japan.Sakurai, 1970, p. 193 Typically, the Naiku ceremony occurs on {{ill|October 2|en|October 2|WD=Q2932|lt=October 2}}, and the Geku ceremony on {{ill|October 5|en|October 5|WD=Q2930|lt=October 5}}, starting at 8 PM.Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 16 == The Ceremony == During Sengyo, the sacred object representing the deity is moved in complete darkness, symbolizing a return to the primordial state before creation. This process involves: * '''Chanting of sacred words''' to mark the deity’s departure from the old shrine. * A '''procession under special canopies''' with accompanying rituals and music. * '''Re-enshrinement''' in the new shrine, with the offering of sacred items and prayers. == Media Coverage == The Sengyo ceremonies are significant cultural events in Japan, often covered extensively by the media. Notably, the 62nd Shikinen Sengu (2013) was broadcast live, reflecting its importance to Japanese cultural heritage.Asahi Shimbun, 2013 == See Also == * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.sengu.info/index.html Ise Shikinen Sengu Official Site] * [http://www.sengu.info/video_sengyo/ Video Footage of Sengyo] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|遷御|version=99285933|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:遷御]] {{wikidata link|Q24892914}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Festivals in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:October]] [[Category:Shinto festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:遷御]] [[ja:遷御]] [[ja:遷御]] {{Shinto ritual | name = Sengyo (遷御) | image = [[File:Ise Shrine Naiku 1953-8-26 cropped.jpg|thumb|220px|旧殿（画像下側）と新殿（画像上側）
第59回（1953rd year ）内宮式年遷宮]] | caption = The transfer ceremony at Ise Grand Shrine during the 59th Shikinen Sengu (1953) | description = A sacred Shinto ritual transferring the deity from an old shrine building to a new one. | associated_shrine = [[Ise Grand Shrine]] | related_event = [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] }} '''Sengyo''' (遷御), or '''Sengyo no Gi''' (遷御の儀), is a crucial [[Shinto]] ritual in the context of the [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] ceremonies at the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It involves the solemn transfer of the deity from the old shrine building to the newly reconstructed one, marking the climax of the Sengu ceremony.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 21-22{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}, 1986, p. 58 == Overview == While general Shinto rites at Ise follow the "Geku-sen-sai" (Outer Shrine first) principle, the Sengyo of the Shikinen Sengu is conducted first at the [[Kōtai Jingū|Naiku]] (Inner Shrine) and then at the [[:en:Geku|Geku]] (Outer Shrine).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai, 1990, p. 150 == Ritual Schedule == Since the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the Sengyo was traditionally held on the 17th day of the 9th lunar month. However, in modern times, the date is determined by the Emperor of Japan.Sakurai, 1970, p. 193 Typically, the Naiku ceremony occurs on {{ill|October 2|en|October 2|WD=Q2932|lt=October 2|1=October 2}}, and the Geku ceremony on {{ill|October 5|en|October 5|WD=Q2930|lt=October 5|1=October 5}}, starting at 8 PM.Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 16 == The Ceremony == During Sengyo, the sacred object representing the deity is moved in complete darkness, symbolizing a return to the primordial state before creation. This process involves: * '''Chanting of sacred words''' to mark the deity’s departure from the old shrine. * A '''procession under special canopies''' with accompanying rituals and music. * '''Re-enshrinement''' in the new shrine, with the offering of sacred items and prayers. == Media Coverage == The Sengyo ceremonies are significant cultural events in Japan, often covered extensively by the media. Notably, the 62nd Shikinen Sengu (2013) was broadcast live, reflecting its importance to Japanese cultural heritage.Asahi Shimbun, 2013 == See Also == * [[Ise Grand Shrine]] * [[Jingū Shikinen Sengū|Shikinen Sengu]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.sengu.info/index.html Ise Shikinen Sengu Official Site] * [http://www.sengu.info/video_sengyo/ Video Footage of Sengyo] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|遷御|version=99285933|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:遷御]] {{wikidata link|Q24892914}} [[Category:Festivals in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Shrine]] [[Category:October]] [[Category:Shinto festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
